preview_text:
The model favors Iran at 48% to win, compared to Egypt's 22% chance. This represents a substantial gap, positioning Iran as the clear statistical favorite on the bias-corrected forecast. Egypt enters as the underdog, though not without meaningful tournament prospects.

The draw probability of 30% reflects a moderately competitive fixture. The spread across outcomes—Iran's near-half likelihood versus Egypt's fifth—suggests the model expects Iran to control much of the match, but the elevated draw chance indicates sufficient competitive balance to make a stalemate plausible rather than a distant outcome.

A marked divergence separates the model and sportsbook consensus. Books and Polymarket both lean heavily toward Egypt at 39% and 39% respectively, versus the model's 22%, while assigning Iran only 26% compared to the model's 48%. The gap exceeds 20 percentage points on Iran's win probability. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these conflicting signals; the discrepancy warrants scrutiny of both methodologies.
